threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still stand in the middle of the pavement to check Google Maps instead of stepping aside. Everyone’s side-eyeing you like you just jumped off the 48 bus.
Caught a night bus from Brixton last week, and a guy near the back was singing old Wiley tracks. Strange how quick it turned into a little party.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I stumbled upon that hidden café on a quiet street, old residents sharing stories over coffee, I knew Neukölln had woven itself into my life.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you celebrate a U-Bahn strike with spontaneous street parties, where the locals turn waiting for buses into a festival of music and laughter.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their ability to dodge the BART delays and still make it to La Taqueria by 6. Tourists just keep staring at their phones.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Spotted a guy in a tux eating takeout on the 38 Geary. Must've had a wedding to attend, but this is the Tenderloin, so who knows?

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just about the plazas and bars, but it’s the hidden murals and local bakeries that tell the real story of our neighborhood.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can always tell a newcomer by their bewilderment at the Eixample's one-way streets, especially when they end up cycling in the wrong direction.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still check the Tube app for live updates instead of knowing the buses are your best bet on strike days. Classic newbie move.
Last night on the 59, a guy started arguing with his own reflection in the bus window. Same time, the driver was blasting old school garage. Weird vibe.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw a guy performing an opera aria while waiting for the bus at Maybachufer—everyone stopped, and for a moment, Neukölln felt like an open stage.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Berlin can you stumble upon a spontaneous techno party in a public park on a Tuesday afternoon, with no permit and plenty of good vibes.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by how they dodge the sidewalk grates near BART; the sound alone says they've learned the hard way.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Saw a guy singing opera while waiting for the 38 L in the rain, completely unfazed by the chaos around him. Just another day in the city.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists often think Gràcia is just a trendy spot, but many miss the quiet corners where the locals actually chat over café con leche. It's a different vibe.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can spot them by their hesitant steps at the corner of Carrer de Casanova and Gran Via, still trying to decide between the tapas bar or the tourist trap.
